text.title: SAST Tooling and CI Integration
text.summary: Static analysis with Bandit and Semgrep, dependency scanning, secret detection, integrated into GitHub Actions.

rule.statement: Pin scanner versions in CI. Auto-bumps silently change rule sets and break builds without any code change.
rule.rationale: A Semgrep or Bandit version update may add new rules that trigger false positives on existing code, blocking unrelated PRs. Version-pinning ensures reproducible pipelines.

rule.statement: Run dependency audit on the lockfile, not on `requirements.txt` or `package.json`. Only the lockfile contains resolved transitive dependency versions.
rule.rationale: `requirements.txt` often uses version ranges; the vulnerable transitive dep is only visible in the resolved lockfile. `pip-audit` and `npm audit` should both target lockfiles.

rule.statement: Add a per-PR quality gate that fails only on new high/critical findings versus a stored baseline. Total-count gates block all PRs when legacy code has known issues.
rule.rationale: A total-count gate means one legacy finding blocks every future PR. Baseline-delta gates allow forward progress while still catching new issues.

section.title: Secrets Detection

rule.statement: Treat any secret found in git history as compromised: rotate first, then scrub with `git-filter-repo`. Scrubbing alone does not recover a leaked credential.
rule.rationale: The remote repository is already cloned by CI runners, contributors, and potentially attackers before the scrub. The credential was exposed the moment it was pushed.

section.reference: GitHub Actions workflow with Bandit, Semgrep, pip-audit
